title: Speech-Language Pathologists
description: Assess and treat persons with speech, language, voice, and fluency disorders. May select alternative communication systems and teach their use. May perform research related to speech and language problems.

tasks.text: Evaluate hearing or speech and language test results, barium swallow results, or medical or background information to diagnose and plan treatment for speech, language, fluency, voice, or swallowing disorders.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write reports and maintain proper documentation of information, such as client Medicaid or billing records or caseload activities, including the initial evaluation, treatment, progress, and discharge of clients.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Monitor patients' progress and adjust treatments accordingly.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or implement treatment plans for problems such as stuttering, delayed language, swallowing disorders, or inappropriate pitch or harsh voice problems, based on own assessments and recommendations of physicians, psychologists, or social workers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Administer hearing or speech and language evaluations, tests, or examinations to patients to collect information on type and degree of impairments, using written or oral tests or special instruments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Educate patients and family members about various topics, such as communication techniques or strategies to cope with or to avoid personal misunderstandings.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise or collaborate with therapy team.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in and write reports for meetings regarding patients' progress, such as individualized educational planning (IEP) meetings, in-service meetings, or intervention assistance team meetings.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Teach clients to control or strengthen tongue, jaw, face muscles, or breathing mechanisms.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct clients in techniques for more effective communication, such as sign language, lip reading, or voice improvement.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Consult with and advise educators or medical staff on speech or hearing topics, such as communication strategies or speech and language stimulation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop speech exercise programs to reduce disabilities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Complete administrative responsibilities, such as coordinating paperwork, scheduling case management activities, or writing lesson plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Consult with and refer clients to additional medical or educational services.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Design, develop, or employ alternative diagnostic or communication devices or strategies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in conferences, training, continuing education courses, or publish research results to share knowledge of new hearing or speech disorder treatment methods or technologies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Use computer applications to identify or assist with communication disabilities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop individual or group activities or programs in schools to deal with behavior, speech, language, or swallowing problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct lessons or direct educational or therapeutic games to assist teachers dealing with speech problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide communication instruction to dialect speakers or students with limited English proficiency.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise students or assistants.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Communicate with students who use an alternative method of communications, using sign language or computer technology.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct or direct research on speech or hearing topics and report findings for use in developing procedures, technologies, or treatments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
